header_tagline: Fast Corpus for European AI teams
summary: Clean PDF and text backlogs to AI-ready JSONL with extracted text, chunks, embeddings, stable IDs, and an inspectable run report at a predictable starting unit price.
positioning: Customers buy completed corpus output and run evidence, not GPU-hours, API/CLI access, or a platform project.
cost_positioning: Cost-effective backlog processing with a scoped run, price per processed page, and a decision based on inspected output.
api_cli_positioning: API/CLI language is integration or handoff context, not the primary public offer.
european_sovereignty_positioning: European execution control and sovereignty are relevant for archives, public-sector teams, research networks, and EU/NL connectors when tied to a real document backlog and pilot decision.
broader_system: Controlled distributed execution for async AI workloads with central scheduling, bounded tasks, retries, visible outcomes, and completed work rather than raw capacity rental.
best_for: teams that want a cost-effective corpus run without building another internal pipeline
archives, libraries, municipalities, research networks, and public-sector teams that want European execution-control context
public or semi-public connector introductions tied to real document-heavy AI workloads
not_for: custom structured extraction in the current trial
highly sensitive data without prior agreement

starting_price.taxes: excluding VAT and taxes unless agreed otherwise
starting_price.qualification: standard clean digital PDF/text batch processing

security_boundary: Low- or moderate-sensitivity pilot workloads. Sensitive or regulated data requires prior agreement. For archive, public-sector, and European AI pilots, execution location, storage location, retention, deletion, and operator access can be made explicit before data is shared; this is governance context, not a blanket compliance or confidentiality guarantee.
